analysis: Seven nights in a row above six and a half hours, with your best night hitting nearly eight — that's not an accident, that's your whole routine doing exactly what it's supposed to do. Your REM is genuinely gorgeous, sitting between one hour thirty-eight and two hours three across the whole stretch, which means your brain is doing its thing and doing it well. The awake time is the one thing worth noticing — that one-hour-seventeen on the tenth and over an hour on the seventh suggests something's pulling you out of sleep mid-night, even when the total looks fine. Deep sleep is running a little lean most nights, hovering in the twenties and thirties of minutes, so if the cold blast and the yin yoga have a stretch goal, that's it.
